title: Writable Layers, Volumes, Bind Mounts, tmpfs, Ownership, and Recovery · Docker Systems Brief: Docker Storage Backup Restore Test
summary: A stateful service loses data after replacement, while a developer bind mount hides packaged files and writes host files as root. Planned artifact: Add and verify the docker storage backup restore test increment in storage selection, ownership, backup, and restore plan. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Quiesce or snapshot consistently, create a bounded backup, verify integrity, restore into a fresh volume, and rehearse application recovery.
Choose tmpfs for bounded ephemeral or sensitive data while accounting for memory, swap, permissions, size, and restart loss.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.config:
# docker-basics cumulative container delivery portfolio
# Browser labs perform deterministic manifest and evidence review only.
# They never connect to a Docker daemon, mount a socket, or execute learner commands.
workspace: docker
version-baseline:
  engine: 29.6.1
  compose: 5.3.1
  buildx: 0.35.0
  buildkit: 0.31.1
  dockerfile-frontend: 1.25
transfer-boundary: authorized-disposable-environment
evidence:

steps.title: Predict Docker Storage Backup Restore Test · Theory Docker Storage Backup Restore Test
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory database writes into an anonymous volume while developers rely on a host-path bind mount. A backup archive exists but has never restored. Define ownership, consistency, lifecycle, checksum, and clean-volume recovery. Competency probe: Quiesce or snapshot consistently, create a bounded backup, verify integrity, restore into a fresh volume, and rehearse application recovery.

Before reading, predict which action preserves the Docker Storage Backup Restore Test contract for the supplied data.

steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for quiesce or snapshot consistently, create a bounded backup, verify integrity, restore into a fresh volume, and rehearse application recovery. For Predict Docker Storage Backup Restore Test · Theory Docker Storage Backup Restore Test, keep Docker Storage Backup Restore Test connected to mechanically reviewable Docker artifact and transfer evidence.

content.title: Reasoning move
content.text: Name client, daemon, builder, image, container, host-kernel, storage, network, registry, and supply-chain boundaries; predict state and authority; review the smallest deterministic manifest change; then verify a failure, repair, cleanup, and live-environment transfer limit.

content.title: Worked case pattern
content.text: A worked example database writes into an anonymous volume while developers rely on a host-path bind mount. A backup archive exists but has never restored. Define ownership, consistency, lifecycle, checksum, and clean-volume recovery. Competency probe: Quiesce or snapshot consistently, create a bounded backup, verify integrity, restore into a fresh volume, and rehearse application recovery. A defensible solution applies Docker Storage Backup Restore Test, preserves the governing invariant or value contract, and connects the observed result to storage selection, ownership, backup, and restore plan.

content.code:
# Evidence: docker-worked-docker-storage-mounts-backup-4
  - competency: docker-worked-docker-storage-mounts-backup-4
    artifact: storage-contract.yaml
    decision: worked docker storage mounts backup 4
    scenario-variant: 3
    environment: air-gapped release worker with an internal registry mirror
    fault-injection: rebuild after the approved base digest is withdrawn
    retained-proof: accessible recovery runbook with rollback rehearsal timing
    case-key: worked4
    change: |
      mount-type: named-volume
      target: /var/lib/intake
      ownership: 10001:10001
      backup: application-consistent
      restore-test: fresh-volume
    verify: docker volume inspect intake-data && sha256sum restore-manifest.txt
    failure: Reject an anonymous volume as a named backup plan, bind-mount portability assumptions, or archive creation without a restore rehearsal.
    repair: Name ownership and lifecycle, quiesce or coordinate the application, checksum the artifact, and restore into a clean isolated volume.
content.caption: Runnable Docker artifact and transfer-evidence contract example; predict its result before running, then change one boundary input.

content.title: Non-worked case
content.text: A successful archive command proves the application can restore consistent data. This fails the Docker Storage Backup Restore Test contract because it omits a precondition, invariant, ownership rule, or counterexample needed by this exact case.

content.label: Mastery evidence
content.value: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Quiesce or snapshot consistently, create a bounded backup, verify integrity, restore into a fresh volume, and rehearse application recovery.
